nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English:  see  Warr  .
2  English:  see  Ware  .

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.
English: variant of   Ware  .

variants.name: Ware
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a name given to a child of either sex baptized in holy water that was, purportedly at least, brought from the River Jordan, whose Hebrew name, ha-yarden, means ‘flowing down’. It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. It is more popular as a boy's name in Britain and as a girl's name in the United States.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Jordan de La Warre was born in 1176, in Wickwar, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de la Warre, was 12 and his mother, Joan de Gresley, was 21. He had at least 2 sons with Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. He died in September 1231, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Brislington, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jordan de La Warre est né en 1176, à Wickwar, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de la Warre, avait 12 ans et sa mère, Joan de Gresley, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. Il est décédé en septembre 1231, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Brislington, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jordan de La Warre 1176 in Wickwar, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de la Warre 12 und seine Mutter Joan de Gresley 21. Er hatte mit Mrs. Jordan de la Warre mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb im September 1231 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Brislington, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jordan de La Warre è nato il nel 1176 nel Wickwar, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de la Warre, aveva 12 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Gresley, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. Morì nel settembre 1231, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Brislington, Somerset, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Jordan de La Warre(이)가 1176년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 윅워에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de la Warre(은)는 12세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Gresley(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Mrs. Jordan de la Warre(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1231년 9월 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 브리슬링턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jordan de La Warre nasceu em 1176, em Wickwar, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de la Warre, tinha 12 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Gresley, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. Ele faleceu em setembro de 1231, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Brislington, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jordan de La Warre родился в 1176 г. в местности викквар, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de la Warre было 12 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Gresley, 21. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. Он умер сентябрь 1231 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности брислингтон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jordan de La Warre nació en 1176, en Wickwar, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de la Warre, tenía 12 años y su mamá, Joan de Gresley, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Mrs. Jordan de la Warre. Murió en septiembre de 1231, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Brislington, Somerset, Inglaterra.
